description: Track B — General Public path. 7 adaptive questions. Produces an AI Personality Type based on usage awareness, comfort level, sentiment, and self-identity.

questions.question: Quick reality check — how often do you actually run into AI in your daily life?

options.label: Barely ever — I don't really think about it
Sometimes — mostly Siri, Google Maps, or Netflix recommendations
Pretty often — I use Claude, ChatGPT, or similar tools a few times a week
Constantly — AI is woven into most of what I do online

questions.question: If someone handed you a new AI app and said "just figure it out" — what happens?

options.label: Hard pass — I'd need a tutorial and probably some hand-holding
I'd give it a shot but would likely need help along the way
I'd poke around and figure it out on my own, no problem
I'd have a workflow figured out before most people open the homepage

questions.question: If AI could take one annoying thing off your plate starting today, what would you pick?

options.label: Managing my inbox and emails
Meal planning, grocery lists, and recipes
Scheduling, calendar coordination, and reminders
Research — finding and summarizing information fast
Creative stuff — writing, brainstorming, or design

questions.question: What's your honest reaction when you see a headline about AI?

options.label: Mostly anxiety — it feels like it's moving too fast
Skeptical — a lot of hype, not enough substance
Interested — I try to stay informed even when it feels overwhelming
Excited — I actually read the article and look for ways to try it

questions.question: What's your biggest hesitation about using AI more in your life?

options.label: I'm not sure I can trust what it tells me
I'm worried about privacy and where my data goes
It feels too complicated to learn or set up
I'm not convinced it would actually help me
Honestly, I just haven't made the time to figure it out

questions.question: When was the last time you tried something new with AI?

options.label: Never — I haven't tried AI at all
Months ago — I tried it once and moved on
In the last few weeks — I poke around when something catches my eye
This week — I'm actively experimenting

questions.question: Would you trust AI to help you make a real decision — like where to invest money, what job to take, or how to handle a tough conversation?

options.label: No way — those decisions are too important for AI
I'd ask it for information, but the decision is mine
I'd seriously consider its perspective alongside my own
I already do this — AI is one of the first places I go when I need to think something through

questions.question: You just found an AI tool that could save you an hour a day. How do you learn it?

options.label: YouTube video — show me, don't tell me
Just open it and start clicking around
Read a guide at my own pace with coffee
Have someone walk me through it live
Watch a friend use it first, then copy what works

questions.question: Last one — which of these sounds most like you right now?

options.label: Curious outsider — I know AI is a thing, I'm just starting to explore
Casual dabbler — I use a few AI tools but haven't gone deep
Active learner — I'm trying new AI tools and staying up on what's changing
Early adopter — I'm usually ahead of most people when new tech drops

scoring.note: Max 125. Boundaries derived from answer profiles: all-A=0, all-B=21, all-C=84, all-D=125. Tiers sit at natural behavioral divides.
